chunk_summary: Vitamin A prophylaxis in India targets children aged 9 months to 5 years, with scheduled doses every 6 months. Catch-up decisions prioritize documentation and program linkage to avoid repeated massive dosing.
learning_objectives: Understand the national Vitamin A prophylaxis schedule for children aged 9 months to 5 years in India.
Learn catch-up decision rules for uncertain dose histories in Vitamin A supplementation.
Differentiate between prophylactic and therapeutic Vitamin A dosing in clinical practice.
key_facts: Vitamin A prophylaxis starts at 9 months with a dose of 1 lakh IU (100,000 IU).
Subsequent doses are 2 lakh IU (200,000 IU) every 6 months from 16–18 months onward.
A total of nine doses are administered by the age of 5 years in the routine schedule.
Catch-up decisions prioritize documentation and linking families to program sites for schedule correction.
Therapeutic dosing for ocular signs of deficiency or measles is separate from prophylaxis and must be documented to prevent repeat dosing.
reference_quote: Vitamin A prophylaxis in India is delivered as a national program from 9 months to 5 years. First dose at 9 months is 1 lakh IU (100,000 IU), followed by 2 lakh IU (200,000 IU) every 6 months.
document_key_topics: Vitamin A prophylaxis schedule in India
Catch-up decisions for missed or undocumented doses
Differentiation between prophylaxis and therapeutic dosing
